preview_text:
The model rates Austria as a moderate favorite, assigning a 45% win probability, while Jordan has a 24% chance of victory. The gap between the two is material but not decisive; Austria does not emerge as a heavy favorite in the model's assessment.

The forecast suggests a relatively competitive match. The draw probability sits at 31%, indicating that a stalemate remains a plausible outcome—nearly as likely as an Austria win in the model's view. The win probabilities are spread across all three results without extreme concentration, pointing to genuine uncertainty rather than a heavily lopsided contest.

The sportsbook consensus and Polymarket both diverge substantially from the model, both favoring Austria at 72%. The books assign only a 10% chance to Jordan, compared to the model's 24%, a gap of 14 percentage points. The model and market are not aligned here; the dashboard makes no claim about which assessment is more accurate, only that they differ meaningfully on this fixture.
